page_title: Study permit: Get the right documents - Canada.ca

h2: Get your letter of acceptance Always required
Apply for a provincial or territorial attestation letter Needed in most cases
Show proof of identity Always required
Show proof of funds Always required
Write a letter of explanation Recommended
Get a medical exam Check if needed
Get a police certificate Check if needed
Proof of care (for minor children in Canada) Check if needed
Check for other documents you might need based on what country you’re applying from Check if needed

sections.heading: Get your letter of acceptance Always required
items: Your school will give you a letter of acceptance. Upload this to your online application.

sections.heading: Apply for a provincial or territorial attestation letter Needed in most cases
items: Get a provincial or territorial attestation letter (PAL/TAL) from your school. In most cases, you need to accept your offer and pay your tuition fees, either in part or in full, to get your PAL/TAL.
If you’re studying in Quebec , you need a Quebec Acceptance Certificate ( Certificat d’acceptation du Québec [CAQ] ).

sections.heading: Show proof of identity Always required
items: To show proof of identity, you (and each family member who comes with you) must have
bullets: a copy of a valid passport or travel document Upload a copy of the information page of your passport when you apply online. If you’re approved , you must send us your original passport.
Upload a copy of the information page of your passport when you apply online. If you’re approved , you must send us your original passport.
If you’re approved , you must send us your original passport.
2 recent passport size photos The name and date of birth of the person must be written on the back of each photo.
The name and date of birth of the person must be written on the back of each photo.

sections.heading: Show proof of funds Always required
items: To support yourself and any family members coming with you, you must show proof of funds .

sections.heading: Write a letter of explanation Recommended
items: You must explain why you want to study in Canada, and that you understand your responsibilities as an international student in Canada.

sections.heading: Get a medical exam Check if needed
items: You may need a medical exam if any of these situations apply to you:
Check if you need a medical exam .
bullets: You plan to stay in Canada for more than 6 months.
You recently spent time in certain countries.
You plan to work in certain jobs where public health must be protected.

sections.heading: Get a police certificate Check if needed
items: In some cases, we may ask you for a police certificate . If you think you might need one, you should apply for one as early as possible .

sections.heading: Proof of care (for minor children in Canada) Check if needed
items: Children under the age of 17 who are not coming to Canada with a parent or legal guardian must have an appointed custodian.
Find out what you need to submit for children under the age of 17 .

sections.heading: Check for other documents you might need based on what country you’re applying from Check if needed
items: There may be other documents to submit that are specific to your local visa office. To get your local visa office instructions:
If you aren’t currently in your home country, you may have to prove your immigration status in the country you apply from.
If the government that issued your passport or travel document needs you to have a re-entry permit, you must get one before you apply for a Canadian visa. You may also need other documents.
bullets: Go to the How to apply page
Select your country or territory from the list.

eligibility_signals: proof of ties to home country
